pages.full_text:
We got to the Tunnel an hour early & they put us onto the next train - ahead of the Bank Holiday weekend.
We drove past Etaples + Rouen + are camping beyond Dreux at a Municipal Park at St. Remysur l'Avre. The river splits in 2 in the town.
A lovely, au warm evening walk in the Sunshine. There's an Irishman in the camp who sits in his car all day.
Delicious
    eclairs
1/3 fields oil seed rape
[illegible] [illegible]
pages.notes: Page is a continuation from preceding spread; top text begins mid-sentence. No handwritten page number visible at top left. Left margin features a pencil sketch of a whisk. Centre-right has a glued-in or rubbed print image of a baguette. Bottom text partially cut off; reads '1/3 fields oil seed rape' then further illegible text. 'au warm' transcribed as written - 'au' may be an informal French usage. Place name 'St. Remysur l'Avre' likely 'St. Remy-sur-Avre' written without hyphens; could also read 'St. Remysur l'Arve'. Right edge of the image is cut off; partial text visible on right margin belongs to right page.

pages.full_text:
[?Bble] Saturday.
      We shopped in Dreux + lunched overlooking R. le [?lour] a few kilometres north of Chateaudun. We've camped at le [?Floe] at Beaugency. lots of watermills, broad river. Old bridges. Church has a capitol of David & Goliath & font from 1593. A good meal (-except pudding). A nice town to wander in.

      Lovely views from the van across the river.

__Sunday__ a near-perfect day so far.
Warm sunshine in the van first thing. An 11 mile bike ride to Meung. lovely bike track. Fields, villages.
Delicious lasagna, rose + lashings of chocolate on the profiteroles at this restaurant. Frogs making a tremendous racket.
A market in Meung + beatiful Romanesque door outside church + nice Stained glass window.
Back to Beaugency. The bridge is beautifal.
Both campsites have been only £11 per night.
photo_captions: Restaurant L'Atelier de Jeanne, 3 impasse du Chateau - Meung sur Loire
pages.notes: Page begins with '[?Bble] Saturday.' - first word before Saturday is unclear, possibly a day abbreviation or page label. No handwritten page number visible at top right. 'R. le [?lour]' is a partial river name - likely 'R. le Loir' (the Loir river). 'le [?Floe]' is uncertain spelling for a campsite at Beaugency - could be 'le Floc' or 'le Floe'. 'Sunday' appears underlined as a section heading. 'beatiful' and 'beautifal' are transcribed as written (diary spellings). A restaurant business card is glued in on the right side: 'Restaurant L'Atelier de Jeanne, 3 impasse du Chateau - Meung sur Loire, www.atelierdejeanne.fr, latelierdejeanne@orange.fr, 02 38 66 12 33 - 06 89 21 20 21'. The price '£11 per night' uses pound sterling symbol (not euro). Volume context (2003-2019) and Bank Holiday reference suggest a May Bank Holiday trip to France.
